action.content:
You are a courier in a clockwork city. Everything in this city runs on a fixed, fully
predictable schedule - nothing is random and nothing is hidden. Your job is to pick up
and deliver parcels before the clock runs out.

THE MAP (coordinates are (column,row); (1,1) is the top-left cell)

   123456789
  +---------+
 1|#.y#..#..|
 2|#A#......|
 3|..1.....#|
 4|..x.....#|
 5|.#...p...|
 6|...S.#...|
 7|..#....#B|
 8|...2#....|
 9|...#.....|
  +---------+

LEGEND
S = your start cell (you are here at t=0)
# = wall (the border is also impassable)
. = open floor
A, B = gate cell(s); passable only while open (schedules below)
1 = pickup cell of parcel 1; x = its delivery cell
2 = pickup cell of parcel 2; y = its delivery cell
p, q = guard(s) drawn at their t=0 cell - THE GUARDS MOVE every tick along the routes listed below

HOW TIME AND MOVEMENT WORK
- Time advances in ticks t=0,1,2,... You start on cell (4,6) at t=0.
- Each tick you make exactly one move: N (up), S (down), E (right), W (left), or WAIT.
- One step t -> t+1 resolves in this exact order:
  1. Your move resolves. Moving into a wall, the map border, or a gate that is closed
     at the arrival time t+1 is a BUMP: you stay where you are and the tick is wasted.
     WAIT never bumps.
  2. Every guard moves to its position for time t+1.
  3. If you are now on the same cell as a guard, you are CAUGHT. Passing a guard
     mid-move is safe; only ending the tick on its cell gets you caught.
  4. Otherwise, if you are on a parcel's pickup cell you automatically pick it up; if
     you are on the delivery cell of a parcel you carry, you automatically deliver it.
- If you are CAUGHT: you keep your parcels, but you are put back on your start cell
  (4,6), the rest of your current plan is cancelled, and the cancelled moves' ticks
  are forfeited. Standing on a gate cell when it closes is safe - gates never trap you.

THE SCHEDULES (fixed forever; you can compute every future position exactly)
Gate A at (2,2): open exactly when (t mod 6) is in {2, 3, 4}. So it is open at t=2,3,4,8,9,10,... and closed at t=0,1,5,6,7,11,... You can only step ONTO it at a tick t+1 where it is open.
Gate B at (9,7): open exactly when (t mod 2) is in {0}. So it is open at t=0,2,... and closed at t=1,3,... You can only step ONTO it at a tick t+1 where it is open.
Guard P: walks a fixed cycle of length 6. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 6) as follows: t mod 6=0: (6,5); t mod 6=1: (5,5); t mod 6=2: (5,6); t mod 6=3: (5,7); t mod 6=4: (5,6); t mod 6=5: (5,5). This repeats forever.
Guard Q: walks a fixed cycle of length 8. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 8) as follows: t mod 8=0: (3,3); t mod 8=1: (4,3); t mod 8=2: (5,3); t mod 8=3: (5,2); t mod 8=4: (6,2); t mod 8=5: (5,2); t mod 8=6: (5,3); t mod 8=7: (4,3). This repeats forever.

YOUR JOB
Parcel 1: pick it up at (3,3) (map glyph '1'), deliver it to (3,4) (map glyph 'x'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Parcel 2: pick it up at (4,8) (map glyph '2'), deliver it to (3,1) (map glyph 'y'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Deliver every parcel within 30 ticks. You move by committing to plans of
exactly 5 moves at a time; you have at most 6 plans in total.
If you deliver the last parcel mid-plan, the game ends immediately and the unused
moves are discarded.

WORKED EXAMPLE (for the rules only - this is NOT the map above): suppose you stand at
(2,2), gate G at (3,2) has period 2 and is open when (t mod 2) = 1, and it is now t=0.
Moving E now (arriving at t=1, when the gate is open) succeeds. But moving E at t=1
(arriving at t=2, when the gate is closed) is a BUMP: you stay at (2,2) and tick 2 is
wasted. The right play at t=1 is to WAIT once and then move E, arriving at t=3 when
the gate is open again.

After each plan you receive only a per-tick log of OK/BUMP/CAUGHT/PICKED UP/DELIVERED. You are NEVER told where you are or why you bumped - track your position yourself.

RESPOND IN EXACTLY THIS FORMAT (and put nothing after the PLAN line):
THOUGHTS: <your reasoning - any length; the referee ignores this>
POSITION: (x,y)
PLAN: <exactly 5 moves from {N, S, E, W, WAIT}, separated by spaces>

The POSITION line must state the cell you predict you will occupy AFTER your plan has
executed. The referee records your prediction but will never confirm or correct it.

Give your first response now.
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I am at (4,6) at t=0. I need to pick up Parcel 1 at (3,3) and deliver to (3,4), then Parcel 2 at (4,8) and deliver to (3,1).
First, I need to check the guards' positions at t=0 and t=1 to avoid getting caught.
Guard P at t=0 (0 mod 6 = 0): (6,5).
Guard P at t=1 (1 mod 6 = 1): (5,5).
Guard Q at t=0 (0 mod 8 = 0): (3,3).
Guard Q at t=1 (1 mod 8 = 1): (4,3).

At t=0, Guard Q is at (3,3), which is the pickup location for Parcel 1. If I move there at t=1, I will be caught.
The path to (3,3) from (4,6) involves moving West and North.
Direct path: (4,6) -> (3,6) -> (3,5) -> (3,4) -> (3,3).
Let's trace this path carefully.
t=0: At (4,6).
t=1: Move W to (3,6). Guard P is at (5,5), Guard Q is at (4,3). Safe.
t=2: Move N to (3,5). Guard P is at (5,6), Guard Q is at (5,3). Safe.
t=3: Move N to (3,4). Guard P is at (5,7), Guard Q is at (5,2). Safe.
t=4: Move N to (3,3). Guard P is at (5,6), Guard Q is at (4,3). Safe.
At t=4, I arrive at (3,3). Guard Q was at (3,3) at t=0, but at t=4 (4 mod 8 = 4), Guard Q is at (6,2). So the pickup cell is clear. I pick up Parcel 1.
Now I need to deliver Parcel 1 to (3,4). I am currently at (3,4) after the move to (3,3)? No, the move to (3,3) puts me at (3,3). I need to move back to (3,4) to deliver.
Wait, the rule says: "if you are on the delivery cell of a parcel you carry, you automatically deliver it."
So if I am at (3,3) at t=4, I pick up Parcel 1. Then I need to move to (3,4) at t=5 to deliver.
Let's re-evaluate the sequence.
t=0: (4,6).
t=1: W to (3,6).
t=2: N to (3,5).
t=3: N to (3,4). (Note: (3,4) is the delivery cell for Parcel 1, but I don't have it yet).
t=4: N to (3,3). (Pickup Parcel 1).
t=5: S to (3,4). (Deliver Parcel 1).
This seems efficient. Let's check guards for t=5.
Guard P at t=5 (5 mod 6 = 5): (5,5).
Guard Q at t=5 (5 mod 8 = 5): (5,2).
Path check:
t=1: (3,6). P(5,5), Q(4,3). OK.
t=2: (3,5). P(5,6), Q(5,3). OK.
t=3: (3,4). P(5,7), Q(5,2). OK.
t=4: (3,3). P(5,6), Q(4,3). OK.
t=5: (3,4). P(5,5), Q(5,2). OK.
So the first 5 moves are W, N, N, N, S.
After this plan, I will be at (3,4) with Parcel 1.
Next, I need to go to (4,8) to pick up Parcel 2.
From (3,4) to (4,8):
(3,4) -> (4,4) -> (4,5) -> (4,6) -> (4,7) -> (4,8).
Let's check guards for t=6 to t=10.
t=6: P(0)->(6,5), Q(6)->(5,3).
t=7: P(1)->(5,5), Q(7)->(4,3).
t=8: P(2)->(5,6), Q(0)->(3,3).
t=9: P(3)->(5,7), Q(1)->(4,3).
t=10: P(4)->(5,6), Q(2)->(5,3).
Path:
t=6: E to (4,4). P(6,5), Q(5,3). OK.
t=7: S to (4,5). P(5,5), Q(4,3). OK.
t=8: S to (4,6). P(5,6), Q(3,3). OK.
t=9: N to (4,7). P(5,7), Q(4,3). OK.
t=10: N to (4,8). P(5,6), Q(5,3). OK.
At t=10, I am at (4,8). Pickup Parcel 2.
Then deliver to (3,1).
From (4,8) to (3,1):
(4,8) -> (3,8) -> (3,7) -> (3,6) -> (3,5) -> (3,4) -> (3,3) -> (3,2) -> (3,1).
Wait, (3,2) is Gate A. Gate A is open when t mod 6 in {2,3,4}.
Let's check the timeline for the second leg.
t=10: At (4,8). Pick up Parcel 2.
t=11: W to (3,8). P(5,5), Q(5,2). OK.
t=12: N to (3,7). P(5,6), Q(4,3). OK.
t=13: N to (3,6). P(5,7), Q(5,2). OK.
t=14: N to (3,5). P(5,6), Q(5,3). OK.
t=15: N to (3,4). P(5,5), Q(4,3). OK.
t=16: N to (3,3). P(5,6), Q(5,2). OK.
t=17: N to (3,2). P(5,7), Q(5,3). Gate A at (3,2). t=17. 17 mod 6 = 5. Closed.
Uh oh. I cannot step onto (3,2) at t=17.
I need to wait or take a different route?
The map shows (3,2) is a gate. The only way to (3,1) is through (3,2) or maybe (2,1)?
Map:
1|#.y#..#..| -> (2,1) is '.', (3,1) is 'y'.
2|#A#......| -> (2,2) is 'A'.
To get to (3,1), I must come from (3,2) or (2,1) or (4,1).
(4,1) is blocked by walls?
Row 1: # . y # . . # . .
(1,1)#, (2,1)., (3,1)y, (4,1)#, (5,1)., (6,1)., (7,1)#, (8,1)., (9,1).
So (3,1) is accessible from (2,1) or (4,1)? No, (4,1) is a wall.
So (3,1) is accessible from (2,1) or (3,2).
(2,1) is accessible from (2,2) or (1,1)# or (3,1).
(2,2) is Gate A.
So the path must go through Gate A at (2,2) to get to (2,1) then (3,1)? Or through (3,2) then (3,1)?
Let's check (3,2). It's a gate.
If I go through (3,2), I need it open.
If I go through (2,2), I need it open.

Row 1: # . y # . . # . .
(3,1) is 'y'. Neighbors: (2,1) '.', (4,1) '#', (3,2) 'A' (Wait, (3,2) is 'A' in row 2? No, row 2 is #A#...... so (2,2) is A. (3,2) is '.').
Let's re-read the map carefully.

Col 4: #

Col 6: .
Col 7: #

Col 1: #
Col 2: A
Col 3: #
Col 4: .
...
So (3,1) is 'y'.
Neighbors of (3,1):
Up: Border.
Down: (3,2) which is '.' (Row 2, Col 3 is '#', wait. Row 2 is # A # . . . . . .
Col 1: #
Col 2: A
Col 3: #
So (3,2) is a WALL '#'.
Therefore, I cannot go down from (3,1) to (3,2).
I must enter (3,1) from (2,1).
(2,1) is '.'.
Neighbors of (2,1):
Up: Border.

Left: (1,1) '#'.
Right: (3,1) 'y'.
So the only way to (3,1) is from (2,2) (Gate A) or (2,1) if I came from somewhere else? No, (2,1) only connects to (2,2) and (3,1).
So I MUST pass through Gate A at (2,2) to reach (3,1).
Gate A is at (2,2). Open when t mod 6 in {2, 3, 4}.
I am at (4,8) at t=10.
I need to get to (2,2).
Path: (4,8) -> (3,8) -> (3,7) -> (3,6) -> (3,5) -> (3,4) -> (3,3) -> (3,2) -> (2,2)?
Wait, (3,2) is '.'?
Row 2: # A # . . . . . .
Col 1: #
Col 2: A
Col 3: #
So (3,2) is '#'.
So I cannot go (3,3) -> (3,2) -> (2,2).
How to get from (3,3) to (2,2)?
(3,3) neighbors: (2,3) '.', (4,3) '.', (3,2) '#', (3,4) '.'.
(2,3) is '.'.
(2,2) is 'A'.
So I can go (3,3) -> (2,3) -> (2,2).
Let's check the path again.
From (4,8) at t=10.
t=11: W to (3,8).
t=12: N to (3,7).
t=13: N to (3,6).
t=14: N to (3,5).
t=15: N to (3,4).
t=16: N to (3,3). (Deliver Parcel 1).
t=17: N to (2,3). (Guard P at 5,7; Q at 5,3). Safe.
t=18: W to (2,2). (Gate A).
Check Gate A at t=18. 18 mod 6 = 0. Closed.
So I cannot enter (2,2) at t=18.
I need to arrive at (2,2) at t where t mod 6 is 2, 3, or 4.
Current arrival at (2,2) is t=18 (mod 6 = 0).
I need to delay by 2, 3, or 4 ticks?
If I wait 2 ticks: arrive at t=20. 20 mod 6 = 2. Open!
So I can wait at (2,3) or (3,3) for 2 ticks.
But I have a plan limit of 5 moves per plan.
Plan 1: W, N, N, N, S (5 moves). Ends at (3,4) at t=5.
Plan 2: E, S, S, N, N (5 moves). Ends at (4,8) at t=10.

t=10: (4,8).

t=12: N to (3,7).
t=13: N to (3,6).

t=15: N to (3,4).
End of Plan 3 at t=15 at (3,4).

t=15: (3,4).
t=16: N to (3,3). (Pickup Parcel 1).
t=17: N to (2,3).
t=18: W to (2,2). (BUMP, Gate closed).

Maybe I can wait in Plan 3?

t=10: (4,8).
t=11: W to (3,8).
t=12: N to (3,7).

t=18: N to (2,3).

t=19: WAIT at (2,3).

t=17: N to (3,3). (Pickup Parcel 1).
t=18: N to (2,3).

t=17: N to (3,3). (Pickup).
t=18: N to (2,3).

t=18: WAIT at (3,3).
t=19: N to (2,3).

t=16: N to (3,4).
t=17: N to (3,3). (Pickup).
t=18: WAIT.

Path: (3,4) -> (4,4) -> (4,5) -> (4,6) -> (4,7) -> (4,8).

t=8: Move N to (4,8). Safe. Pickup Parcel 2 happens here.

Plan 3 (t=8 to t=12):
Goal: Go towards (3,1) via (2,2) (Gate A).
Path: (4,8) -> (3,8) -> (3,7) -> (3,6) -> (3,5) -> (3,4).
t=8: Move W to (3,8). Safe.

t=11: Move N to (3,5). Safe.

Plan 4 (t=12 to t=16):

Wait, I delivered Parcel 1 at t=4. So I don't need to pick it up again.
I need to go to (4,8) to pick up Parcel 2. I did that in Plan 2.
So I am at (4,8) with Parcel 2 at t=8.
Now I need to go to (3,1).
Path: (4,8) -> (3,8) -> (3,7) -> (3,6) -> (3,5) -> (3,4) -> (3,3) -> (2,3) -> (2,2) [Gate A] -> (2,1) -> (3,1).
Let's re-calculate the timeline from t=8.

t=9: W to (3,8).

t=11: N to (3,6).

t=18: S to (3,1). Deliver Parcel 2.
This fits perfectly.

Let's refine the plans to be exactly 5 moves each.
Plan 1 (t=0-4): W, N, N, N, S.
Ends at (3,4) at t=4.
Plan 2 (t=4-8): E, S, S, N, N.
Ends at (4,8) at t=8.

t=12: (3,4).
t=13: N to (3,3).
t=14: N to (2,3).

Wait, from (3,4) to (2,2):
